concerns.text: FR-001 mandates querying the Materials Project API for 'required variables' (including ductility), but the 'Assumptions' section explicitly states: 'The Materials Project API... does not expose experimentally measured ductility... Therefore, the system MUST rely exclusively on the HuggingFace... collection... treating Materials Project data as a supplementary source for alloy crystallographic descriptors only.' This creates a direct contradiction where an FR requires fetching data that the spec admits does not exist in that source.

FR-010 states: 'System MUST train a gradient‑boosting regressor (XGBoost) on [deferred] of the data... and test on a [deferred] hold‑out set'. The use of '[deferred]' as a literal value for the data split definition (e.g., '70%') violates the spec's own 'Assumptions' which state 'CPU‑only XGBoost... will converge on ≤ 250 records within the 6‑hour CI time budget' and the 'Methodology sketch' which specifies '70%... 15%... 15%'. The requirement is ambiguous and contradicts the fixed split strategy defined elsewhere in the document.

In US-3 Acceptance Scenario 2, the text says: 'returns a ductility prediction within 0.1 % of the model’s internal prediction time (< 2 seconds)'. This phrasing is logically incoherent. A prediction value (ductility %) cannot be 'within 0.1% of' a time duration (seconds). It likely intends to say the prediction *latency* is within 0.1% of the 2-second limit, or the prediction *value* is within 0.1% of the ground truth. As written, it compares incompatible units.

FR-007 requires the pipeline to 'abort' if partial R² < 0.50. However, SC-001 and US-2 define this as a success criterion for the *model*, not a hard stop for the *system*. If the data simply does not support R² ≥ 0.50, aborting prevents the generation of the diagnostic report required by FR-013 and SC-005. The requirement to 'abort' contradicts the requirement to 'generate a final report' (FR-013) which must include the results of the analysis even if it fails the threshold.
